frameworks.name: invariant object representation
frameworks.one_line: Categorisation relies on representations invariant to feature transformations

frameworks.name: spatial scaffolding of temporal structure
frameworks.one_line: Space provides a representational substrate that supports temporal generalisation

frameworks.name: relational feature-manifold learning
frameworks.one_line: Objects defined by sequential transitions across a 2D feature manifold

core_question: How are rotation-invariant representations learned for objects defined by relations among features in time?
key_assumptions: Invariances can be learned by explicitly coding spatial relations among features
Temporal objects can be operationalised as sequences across a 2D feature manifold
Transfer to rotated transition vectors indexes rotation-invariance
manipulations: Modality of temporal-object features: auditory vs visual vs spatial
Prior association of non-spatial features with physical spatial locations
Rotation of transition vectors across the feature manifold at test
key_findings: Rotation-invariant temporal objects were learned only when features were explicitly spatial
Non-spatial features supported rotation-invariance after prior association with spatial locations
Transfer to novel rotated objects depended on this spatial grounding
interpretations: Space acts as a scaffold for generalising relational information across time
Invariance learning for temporal objects piggybacks on spatial coding mechanisms
